title: weinmannia microphylla var. parvifolia
intro: Weinmannia microphylla var. parvifolia, also known as the New Zealand tōtara, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cunoniaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Weinmannia microphylla var. parvifolia include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin. The bark can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for medicinal use, and it is also used in traditional Maori medicine to promote healing and well-being.
